title: Balance
subtitle: Your planned income and expenses, month by month.

mojibake.confirm: Remove {{count}} line(s) with invalid characters? Re-import the statement afterwards — the accents will come through correctly.
mojibake.alert: {{deleted}} line(s) removed. Import the statement again to recover them with correct accents.
mojibake.banner: <1>{{count}}</1> old import(s) with invalid characters (�). Remove them and re-import the statement to fix the accents.

list.emptyFixedIncome: No fixed income. Add your salary or other recurring inflows.
list.emptyFixedExpenses: No fixed expenses. Add rent, subscriptions, insurance, etc.

donut.emptyFixed: Add fixed expenses to see the breakdown.
donut.emptyVariable: Add variable expenses to see the breakdown.
donut.emptyIncome: Add income to see the breakdown.

uncat.bannerHint: · helps you get a better breakdown of your spending.
uncat.modalIntro: Pick a category for each merchant — it applies to all matching lines at once. Suggestions (<1>✨</1>) come from the automatic dictionary. Leave blank to classify later.

pending.headHint: · choose <1>Fixed</1> or <2>Variable</2>. The app learns the merchant and applies it to matching ones (now and in future imports).

timeline.head: Last {{months}} months
timeline.footer: Expenses shown as negative bars. Blue line = final balance (income − expenses) per month. Months with an imported statement show real values; the rest show the plan.
timeline.footerClick: Click a month to analyse it in detail.

expense.title: {{action}} {{kind}} expense
expense.loanSynced: Amount synced with the loan's payment.

variableMonths.label: THIS MONTH'S MOVEMENTS
variableMonths.removeConfirm: Remove {{count}} transaction(s)? You can re-import them later.
variableMonths.empty: No movements this month. Import a statement or add them manually.

import.title: Import bank statement
import.noTransactions: Couldn't read any transactions from this file. Accepts PDF, CSV or OFX statements.
import.pdfError: Error reading the PDF. Try exporting the statement as CSV/OFX if possible.
import.doneIncomesExpenses: Imported successfully: <1>{{incomes}}</1> income line(s) and <3>{{expenses}}</3> expense line(s).
import.doneMatchedToPlan: {{count}} matched to existing fixed items (not duplicated).
import.doneRemaining: The rest land in <1>To classify</1> — choose <3>Fixed</3> or <5>Variable</5> and the app learns for next time.
import.intro: Select your bank statement as <1>PDF</1>, <3>CSV</3> or <5>OFX</5>. Each line is read and classified as income (in) or expense (out) — we use the <7>transaction</7> amount (not the balance) and you can review everything before importing.
import.privacy: Files are processed in your browser — nothing is sent until you confirm.
import.dupHint: 📌 {{count}} transaction(s) were already imported — unticked automatically.

bank.title: Connect bank
bank.secure1: Uses <1>Open Banking (PSD2)</1>, the European bank-access standard, via GoCardless — an authorised, supervised institution.
bank.secure2: You authenticate <1>on your bank's official site</1> — Wallet360 never sees or stores your credentials.
bank.secure3: Access is <1>read-only</1>: no one can move money.
bank.secure4: Consent lasts <1>90 days</1> and you can revoke it anytime.
bank.notConfigured: Direct connection is almost ready — the (free) Open Banking provider credentials still need enabling on the server. In the meantime you can keep using <1>Import statement</1>.
bank.afterAuth: After authorising on your bank's site, come back here and click <1>⟳ Sync transactions</1>. New entries land in <3>To classify</3>, with learned rules applied automatically.
